Reason for today's visit? Back pain. Location of Pain? Lumber, thighs, legs and feet. Does you pain radiate? If so
where? The pain radiates from the lumbar spine to the feet. What is your current pain on a scale of 0-10? 8. What is
your average pain on a scale of 0-10? 6. Has your pain changed since last office visit? No. What is the frequency of
your pain? Constant. Describe your pain: Aching,Burning,Tingling,Numbness. What makes your pain
worse? Standing, Sitting, Walking, Twisting, Exercises. What makes your pain better? Laying down, medications.
How much pain relief/functional improvement does your current medication provide? 10-30%. Do you feel that you
can use less medication than currently prescribed? No. Are you having any side effects from your
medication? Yes. When was the last time you took pain medication? I have been out of medication.
Are you currently taking Anticoagulants/ Blood Thinners? No. What doctor manages the blood thinner? None. Why
are you taking a blood thinner? None. Have you had any procedures to treat your pain in the last two months? No. If
yes, how much pain relief did you have? NA. Did you have any complications from the procedure? No. Are you
currently attending conservative treatment including PT/Chiro/Massage/Acupuncture? If so which one? No. How many
sessions have you completed? NA. Percentage of relief obtained from conservative treatment? 0%.
Have you completed any Imaging or Diagnostic studies since last office visit? No.
Change in appetite denies. Chills denies. Weight gain denies. Weight loss denies. Weakness denies.
Constipation denies. Diarrhea denies. Nausea denies. Vomiting denies.
Dizziness denies. Fainting denies. Gait abnormality denies.
Surgical History: lumbar discectomy at L5 8/17/2022, back surgery 05/2025, Lumbar laminectomy LEFT L4 and L5
Hospitalization/Major Diagnostic Procedure: fall 2015, denies recent 03/2023.
Migrated Social History: Drugs/Alcohol: (Alcohol Screen): No Did you have a drink containing alcohol in the past

Extremities: No clubbing, Cyanosis or edema
Skin: Warm, dry, intact. no rashes on exposed skin.
MSK: Chronic arthritic changes in multiple joints
Inspection: no muscle atrophy, reversal of normal lordosis and exaggeration of thoracic kyphosis

Facet loading: RIGHT- positive/ LEFT- positive.

History- He incurred pain on August 15, 2015, he slid and fell on the floor when he was mopping. He began to feel
numbness and weakness to his legs. He saw pain management and had two epidural injections. This did not help. He
did physical therapy in 2015 and repeated again this year, the month prior to this initial appointment. He had discectomy
on 8/17/22 at the back institute in Los Angeles. He was searching online and found this surgeon and had great
testimonies. Per Dr. Jagadesh, no surgery indicated. He has more pain now. He cannot stand for longer than 10
minutes whereas before he could for 30 minutes. He is not able to work.
I reviewed CT of the lumbar spine performed on August 16, 2022 which revealed mild posterior disc bulge at multiple
levels. There is mild facet arthropathy from L1-L5. There is moderate canal stenosis at L5-S1 with mild to moderate
bilateral neural foraminal narrowing. At L4-5 there is severe central canal stenosis and moderate bilateral neural
foraminal narrowing. L3-4 there is severe central canal stenosis as well as at L2-3. Reviewed an x-ray of the lumbar
spine which revealed no evidence of fracture. EMG that was performed in 9/30/2022 was normal per patient.
It was recommended that he have a neurological consultation since he has not had relief from spinal injections and was
not a surgical candidate. There may be a neurological condition that may be causing his pain. Pt did have neurology
follow up and had a new LE EMG. Per report he is positive for L L3-4 radiculopathy. This was not identified on his last
patient is a pleasant 43-year-old male who is presenting for lumbar radiculopathy down the back of both legs. He
reports that he did have a left L4 and L5 laminectomy in Africa about a year ago. Reports that he continues to have pain
down both legs. Discussed lumbar MRI in detail today with patient. He would like to proceed with an epidural. He
reports no surgeries at L5 and S1. Discussed ILESI L5/S1 in detail with patient. He would like to proceed. Denies any
use of blood thinners. Reports that he would like to trial physical therapy. He would also like to hold off any surgical
evaluations. Denies neurological deficits, red flag symptoms, or severe pain. ER precautions given. He would like a
Toradol injection today due to pain. He reports that pain is worsened with standing for long period of time, walking for
long period of time, sitting to standing positions as well. No pain in the buttocks or SI joint. Reports that he cannot do
much for over 30 minutes before having pain. Discussed conservative measures and activity modification in detail as
well. He would like to have increase in gabapentin. he would like to trial 400 mg 3 times daily first before increasing any
To: STAR, Subject: Referrals, Fax#: 4809560654, SendDate: 03/30/2026 12:43:41 AM, page 4/10 [-ufg2.14.5in]
Patient Name: BIZIMANA, OLIVIER, DOB: 08/12/1982, Account No: 34131
The patient presents with chronic *BACK* pain radiating into the **lower** extremity in a dermatomal distribution
consistent with [L5/S1] radiculopathy. Pain has been present for approximately > 6 weeks. Physical examination
demonstrates objective evidence of nerve root involvement, including + straight leg raise reproducing radicular
symptoms, decreased sensation in the corresponding dermatome MRI of the lumbar spine dated shows stenosis at L4-5
and L5-S1 with impingement of the nerve roots, correlating with the patient's symptoms and exam findings. The patient
has participated in and remains engaged in conservative management, including a structured physical therapy program
from (core stabilization, stretching, and functional restoration) and an ongoing home exercise program performed at
least 2 times per week for 6 weeks as well as trials of medications such as NSAIDs, neuropathic agents, activity
modification, and use of heat/ice, without adequate or durable relief. Despite this conservative care over more than [6]
weeks, the patient continues to experience function-limiting radicular pain that interferes with
[walking/standing/sitting/sleep/ADLs)] An epidural steroid injection is requested as a medically necessary next step to
reduce nerve root inflammation, improve pain, and facilitate further participation in active rehabilitation. The procedure
will be performed under continuous fluoroscopic guidance with non-ionic contrast dye to confirm correct epidural
placement and spread prior to steroid injection, in accordance with safety and Medicare requirements.
-Lumbar MRI 03/2026 on chart and reviewed with patient in detail.
MRI L/S 4/18/24 attached in pt's chart
MRI L/S 10/4/22: Modic changes noted, full report attached in pt chart.
MRI L/S 10/12/22 (rad impression): L5-S1 broad-based disc protrusion 4 mm with annular visual tear epidural space.
Right neural foraminal narrowing. Grade 1 retrolisthesis of L5 over S1. Full report attached to pt chart, not compaired to
This is a essentially normal study providing no convincing electrophysiological evidence of large fiber peripheral
neuropathy, right cervical/lumbar sacral radiculopathy or upper/lower extremity mono neuropathy to explain patient's
DC pregabalin 100mg caused loss of vision
MDP completed Oct 2022 - no relief of pain
Interventional Procedures: :LEFT L4/5 LAMI PER PT IN AFRICA
8/26/24 B/L L3-5 MBB he reports no relief
- Referred to Dr. Valdes - recommended surgery; pt declined
- Continue with psych for MDD and Gen Anxiety
declines new referral to spine surgeon
The patient's initial evaluation was performed by a physician. Today's visit continues and is consistent with the patient's
previously established plan of care and is billed incident to, as there is a doctor who is immediately available in person
or via tele-communication. The supervising physician, Dr. Haddad, has reviewed the plan of care and is in agreement,
including with the medications prescribed, (if applicable) for this patient.

TTIIS note was transchbed using electronic sonware and voice recognition sonware. TIMS note may contain innerent
spelling, grammatical or dictation inaccuracies due to software. Although proofreading was performed, some errors do
persist. Please feel free to contact our office with any clarification or questions.
OPIATE PAIN MEDICATIONS: The risks of opiate pain medications have been discussed with the patient. These risks
include addiction, medication tolerance, physical dependence, respiratory depression, constipation, nausea, an opioid-
induced hyperalgesic state (increase in pain from opioid medications) and even death. Tolerance can limit the efficacy
of opioids for long-term pain management. The patient has been warned to keep their medication in a safe place,
completely secured from unauthorized access by children or others in the home. The best way to store these
medications is in a lockable container. While taking opiate medications patients are instructed to abstain from using
alcohol. While taking pain medications, patients are cautioned about driving or operating heavy machinery while taking
these medications due to the risk of impairment and motor vehicle accident. They are never to drive if they are impaired
in any way. The patient has signed an opioid treatment agreement and states they are taking the medication as
prescribed. Pharmacy board report reviewed today and consistent with history. At present the patient reports that the
medication significantly improves their pain/quality of life and provides functional improvement. The patient provided
their informed consent to continue opiate therapy as they believe that the benefits outweigh these risks.
As this patient is clearly uncomfortable today, the patient has been provided with a Toradol injection, 30 mg IM to afford
some palliation of the acute pain. The patient tolerated the injection well and was discharged in a stable condition. Vital
Refill Gabapentin Capsule, 400 MG, 1 capsule, Orally, three times a day, 30 days, 90 Capsule, Refills 0, Notes to
Pharmacist: Do not dispense until 30 days after last fill, or on the last business day prior to 30 days in cases where
pharmacy is closed on 30th day. Don't drink alcohol while taking this medication.
ILESI L5/S1 WITH IVCS NO CC
